title: FINS3666 2025 T1 Assessment 2 Group Project Peer Evaluation V2
body:
Peer Evaluation: Assessment 2, Group Project 1. Instructions for Students

An important component of the Group Project is to reflect on the contribution of each member in your team via peer evaluation. The Team Evaluation Tool will become available in Moodle once your Group Project has been submitted. Key points for using the Team Evaluation Tool in Moodle include: • You will see a list of your team members, but you will not rate yourself • Your reviews are NOT anonymous, you will be able to view the scores and comments provided by all reviewers once results are released • You will receive a 10% penalty if you do NOT complete the peer evaluation • For each person in your group, use the (0–6) scale below to rate their overall contribution • The reflective commentary provides an opportunity to provide specific examples to support your scores where applicable • Please double check your responses for fairness and accuracy • Click 'Submit' when you are finished.

Score Contribution Level Grade Equivalent 0 Zero contribution 0% 1 Limited contribution 25% 2 Pass 50% 3 Credit 65% 4 Distinction 75% 5 High Distinction 85% 6 Perfect 100%

Given that your reviews are NOT anonymous, please ensure your feedback is respectful, constructive, honest and be kind to each other. CRICOS Provider Code 00098G  2. Peer Evaluation Rubric: Summary

The following table summarises the peer evaluation feedback required for the Group Project. More detailed and granular criteria are provided below.

Criteria Description Engagement & Attended meetings, contributed ideas and took on tasks Participation (20%) proactively Technical Quality and quantity of input to data analysis, modelling or coding Contribution (20%) Communication & Shared progress clearly, responded to team communications and Collaboration (20%) offered support Timeliness & Met deadlines, completed assigned tasks on time and to the Reliability (20%) expected standard Critical Thinking & Took ownership, raised strategic suggestions, extended ideas Initiative (20%) beyond coursework, e.g. applying AI, ML, advanced risk techniques.

3. Reflective Commentary (Required)

Please answer the following briefly for each group member: • What was this person’s most valuable contribution to the project ? • Were there any challenges working with this person ? If so, how did you address them ? • Should this person’s final grade should differ from the group grade ? Why or why not ?

4. Submission Checklist

• Complete all rubric scores for each group member • Write 2–3 sentences for each reflective commentary • Submit your form individually by the same deadline as the Group Report • Declare any use of AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT) to reflect academic integrity.
